data.body:
PASS 1 (G6 — approach soundness) review of Cluster 1.

Affirmations (criteria this chunk passes):
- G6 FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: the byte-preserving DRY approach directly solves the named problem (five drifted canonical encoders). Verified the drift analysis against source: conflict_resolver._hash_value (conflict_resolver.py:218) omits ensure_ascii → stdlib default True; mutation.serialize_manifest (mutation.py:106) uses ensure_ascii=True; signing._canonical_payload (signing.py:198) and schema.canonical_json (schema.py:396) use ensure_ascii=False. The plan's per-site param choices reproduce each exactly.
- G6 MECHANISM (positional preservation): additive keyword-only params genuinely keep the six positional importers byte-identical — verified today's signature is canonical_str(event) (canonical.py:39).
- G6 APPROACH SELECTION: chosen approach (extend the existing canonical.py seam; PRESERVE each site's ensure_ascii rather than normalize) is defensible vs the two structural alternatives I generated (a separate hash module; normalizing all sites to one ensure_ascii). Refusing to normalize is the correct call for a pure-DRY refactor — normalizing would change conflict_resolver's value_hash / echo-suppression decision. No superior alternative was missed.
- G6 POSITIVE RATIONALE: present and clear (the "Why" section + the explicit no-live-bug framing).

Finding (G6 — mechanism completeness, MINOR):
- [ ] Specify how mutation.serialize_manifest's RETURNED HASH is produced after migration. The function returns a (json_text, sha256_hash) tuple (mutation.py:89,107), but the plan describes the migration only as `canonical_str(items, ascii_only=True)`, which yields just the text. State whether the second tuple element is recomputed locally (hashlib.sha256(json_text.encode())) or routed through the seam's content_hash(items, ascii_only=True) — both reproduce the bytes, but the plan currently under-specifies the hash half of this site's contract.
  location: Cluster 1 "Migrate each duplicate site" → mutation.py bullet; src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/mutation.py:89-107
  evidence: plan says "serialize_manifest → canonical_str(items, ascii_only=True)"; actual return at mutation.py:107 is (json_text, sha256_hash) — the hash element is unaddressed.
  criteria: G6
  suggested_fix: Add to the mutation.py bullet: "serialize_manifest keeps returning (text, hash); compute text via canonical_str(items, ascii_only=True) and the hash via content_hash(items, ascii_only=True) (= sha256 of those exact bytes), deleting the local hashlib.sha256 call."
